## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if the rule is satisfied (and its action should be executed); otherwise, [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Discussion

A rule system calls this method when evaluating its rules.

If the rule was created with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(predicate:assertingFact:grade:)] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(predicate:retractingFact:grade:)], calling this method returns the result of testing the predicate against the provided rule system. If the rule was created with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(blockPredicate:action:)] method, calling this method calls the predicate block and returns the result. # evaluatePredicate(in:)

**Instance Method**

Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the rule has been satisfied in the context of the specified rule system.

## Declaration

```swift
func evaluatePredicate(in system: GKRuleSystem) -> Bool
```

## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if the rule is satisfied (and its action should be executed); otherwise, [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Discussion

A rule system calls this method when evaluating its rules.

If the rule was created with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(predicate:assertingFact:grade:)] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(predicate:retractingFact:grade:)], calling this method returns the result of testing the predicate against the provided rule system. If the rule was created with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(blockPredicate:action:)] method, calling this method calls the predicate block and returns the result. Otherwise, this method always returns [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false]—subclasses should override this method to implement their own predicate tests.

## Evaluating a Rule

- **performAction(in:)**: Performs actions that should result when the rule is satisfied in the context of the specified rule system.
